problem: Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism?
hypothesis: Ketamine-like NMDA antagonism without dissociation may resolve Why do traumatic memories become locked and intrusive — what neural mechanism? by activating at the systems level.

experiment.protocol: Human TMS-EEG study with 32 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Nature uses only the longest threads to weave her patterns. — Richard Feynman
